text.title: Design Doc Advanced Patterns Checklist
text.summary: Phase-by-phase checklist and quality gates for each advanced design section.

list.item: spec.md with FR-X and NFR-X items is approved (human sign-off exists)
base design.md with AD-X architectural decisions is present
constitution.md is accessible for test coverage targets and tech standards
features/done/ reviewed for similar security or performance patterns to reuse
Tree structure uses plain indentation — no ASCII art box-drawing characters
Each component has a TypeScript interface definition (props, optional props marked with ?)
State management approach is documented (local state, context, store)
Component behavior documented: validation triggers, loading states, event handlers
All component file paths specified (e.g., src/components/auth/RegisterForm.tsx)
Every new npm/pip/gem package listed with pinned version range
License noted for each package
External services listed with Required/Optional and local dev fallback
No packages added that duplicate existing dependencies (check constitution.md)
Table columns present: Concern | Mitigation | AD Reference
Mandatory concerns covered: password storage, XSS, CSRF, SQL injection, rate limiting, input validation
Every mitigation references a specific AD-X decision
All security parameters (bcrypt cost, rate limit thresholds) sourced from spec.md NFR-X — none invented
Domain-specific threats considered (business logic abuse, multi-tenant isolation if applicable)
Table columns present: Concern | Strategy | Target | AD Reference
Every target value traced to a specific NFR-X from spec.md
No invented numbers — absent NFR means "TBD — NFR required"
Strategy column is concrete (e.g., "connection pooling, max 20 connections") not generic (e.g., "use caching")
Unit layer: coverage target % (from constitution.md), specific test file paths listed
Integration layer: all API endpoints listed with happy path and at least one error path
E2E layer: critical flows listed including error flows and edge cases (not only happy paths)
Mocking strategy documented (which external services are mocked in tests)

rule.statement: If design.md includes no schema changes, write "No migration required" and skip this section. Do not generate boilerplate migration content for greenfield features.
rule.rationale: Boilerplate migration sections confuse implementers and create unnecessary review overhead.

list.item: Data migration steps (if applicable): numbered, reversible where possible
Backward compatibility: explicitly stated as maintained or broken (with migration path)
Rollout plan: stages with percentages or feature flag names
Rollback procedure: numbered steps to undo the deployment

rule.statement: Design review (human) must occur before impl-plan is written. The advanced sections are the primary review surface. No impl-plan authoring until design has human sign-off.
rule.rationale: Advanced sections (security, migrations) contain the highest-risk design decisions. Reviewing them before implementation prevents costly rework.
